instructions.instruction: Treat consolidation as editorial graph curation rather than bulk clustering or summary writing.
instructions.original_snippets: Consolidation is editorial graph curation, not bulk clustering. ... relation-first rather than summary-first
instructions.relevant_when: When designing or executing diary consolidation workflow artifacts.

instructions.instruction: Default to creating proposed relations and do not auto-accept by default.
instructions.original_snippets: Default rule: create proposals, do not auto-accept. ... Create relations with status: proposed unless the user explicitly asks for acceptance review
instructions.relevant_when: Whenever relation proposals are being created or persisted.

instructions.instruction: Use a bounded working set instead of consolidating the entire diary by default.
instructions.original_snippets: Never consolidate the whole diary by default. ... Start with one bounded slice
instructions.relevant_when: When choosing scope for a consolidation run or dream pass.

instructions.instruction: Generate candidate pairs intentionally rather than using blanket all-vs-all pairing.
instructions.original_snippets: Do not perform blanket pair generation. ... Generate candidate pairs from these signals
instructions.relevant_when: When preparing relation candidates from diary entries.

instructions.instruction: Prefer candidate signals such as shared scope, overlapping refs, temporal adjacency, repeated symptoms, and cross-type sequences.
instructions.original_snippets: Generate candidate pairs from these signals: same scope tag ... overlapping refs ... temporal adjacency ... decision followed by procedural implementation
instructions.relevant_when: When building relation candidates from incidents, decisions, commits, and scan entries.

instructions.instruction: Treat server clustering only as a weak candidate source and not as trusted semantic judgment.
instructions.original_snippets: Server clustering may be used only as a weak candidate source. Treat every cluster suggestion as untrusted until reviewed.
instructions.relevant_when: When a consolidation run includes server-provided clusters or similarity groups.

instructions.instruction: Judge each candidate pair relation-specifically and not with a generic 'related' heuristic.
instructions.original_snippets: For each candidate pair, read both entries ... The judgment must be relation-specific, not 'these feel related'.
instructions.relevant_when: When deciding whether to create supports, elaborates, contradicts, caused_by, references, or supersedes edges.

instructions.instruction: Use contradicts only for substantive incompatibility on the same subject or diagnosis.
instructions.original_snippets: contradicts ... claims cannot both be treated as current truth ... contradiction is substantive, not different emphasis
instructions.relevant_when: When reviewing conflicting incidents, diagnoses, or semantic entries.

instructions.instruction: Use supersedes only when one entry should replace another as the active version.
instructions.original_snippets: supersedes ... source is the new active version of target ... use stricter evidence than for contradicts
instructions.relevant_when: When deciding whether a newer entry replaces an older one rather than merely contradicting or elaborating it.

instructions.instruction: Attach review metadata including rationale, confidence, evidence refs, proposal method, reviewer, and working set to every proposed relation.
instructions.original_snippets: Every created relation must include review metadata ... confidence ... evidenceRefs ... proposalMethod ... rationale ... reviewedAt ... reviewedBy ... workingSet
instructions.relevant_when: When serializing relation proposals or review packets.

instructions.instruction: Report a review packet summarizing working set, candidate count, proposals by type, skips, and open questions.
instructions.original_snippets: At the end of the run, report: working-set definition ... candidate count ... proposals created by relation type ... skipped candidates and why
instructions.relevant_when: When finishing a consolidation batch and presenting results.

instructions.instruction: A dream pass must stay bounded, stop after one pass, and leave all new edges proposed.
instructions.original_snippets: A dream is a bounded background consolidation pass ... stop after one pass ... leave all new edges in proposed
instructions.relevant_when: When designing an autonomous or periodic maintenance workflow.

instructions.instruction: A dream pass must never auto-accept relations, edit source entries, process the entire diary at once, or collapse contradictions into rewritten summaries.
instructions.original_snippets: Never let a dream pass: auto-accept relations ... edit source entries ... process the entire diary at once ... collapse contradictions into one rewritten summary
instructions.relevant_when: When defining the boundaries of a background consolidation routine.

instructions.instruction: Verify consolidation quality by checking compile/search behavior against the same task prompt as before.
instructions.original_snippets: After consolidation, test retrieval quality with diaries_compile or entries_search using the same task prompt as before
instructions.relevant_when: When validating whether a consolidation run improved retrieval quality.
